title: refactor: validation: mark CheckBlockIndex as const
summary: Marks the CheckBlockIndex validation method as const to ensure it doesn't cause side effects during block index verification.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves code safety and clarity by enforcing that CheckBlockIndex cannot modify state, which may help prevent subtle bugs and make the validation code more robust and easier to reason about.

title: rest/rpc: use more util::Join
summary: Replaces manual string joining logic in RPC and REST code with the util::Join utility function, and refactors util::Join to work with range adaptors.
potential_rationale: This change likely reduces code duplication and potential bugs in string handling, while potentially improving performance by minimizing unnecessary container allocations in RPC/REST operations.

title: rpc: refactor: use string_view in Arg/MaybeArg
summary: Refactors RPC helper functions Arg and MaybeArg to use string_view instead of raw pointers, improving the API design.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves memory safety and API usability by eliminating raw pointer handling in RPC argument processing, which may reduce the risk of memory-related bugs and make the code more maintainable.

title: kernel: improve BlockChecked ownership semantics
summary: Changes BlockChecked validation interface to use shared_ptr for better ownership semantics, allowing subscribers to access blocks outside callback scope without copying.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves performance and flexibility for validation interface users by eliminating expensive block copying operations while maintaining memory safety through shared ownership semantics.

title: test: ensure logs are flushed before reading them
summary: Ensures log files are properly flushed before tests attempt to read them, addressing intermittent test failures.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves test reliability by fixing race conditions between log writing and reading operations, which may help reduce flaky test failures in the CI system and local development.

title: doc: use new block_to_connect parameter name
summary: Updates documentation to use the correct parameter name 'block_to_connect' instead of the outdated 'pblock' name. The parameter was previously renamed in the code but the documentation wasn't updated to match.
potential_rationale: This may help improve developer experience by ensuring documentation accurately reflects the codebase, which could reduce confusion for contributors working with block connection functionality.

title: cmake: make missing Python interpreter behaviour more explicit
summary: Improves CMake build system behavior when Python interpreter is missing by adding explicit options and fatal errors instead of silent failures. Adds a BUILD_FUNCTIONAL_TESTS option and makes Python requirements more explicit.
potential_rationale: This likely helps prevent confusing build failures and makes Python dependencies clearer, which may improve the developer experience and reduce support burden from unclear error messages.

title: cmake: fatal error when PIE not supported
summary: Changes CMake build system to throw fatal errors when Position Independent Executable (PIE) support is missing, while also respecting explicit user preferences to disable PIE. Previously this would only show warnings.
potential_rationale: This appears to strengthen security by making it harder to accidentally build without PIE protection, while still allowing knowledgeable users to explicitly disable it. PIE likely helps protect against certain types of memory-based attacks.

title: kernel: make blockTip index const
summary: Makes the blockTip index parameter const in the kernel notification interface. This prevents subscribers from mutating the index while still allowing them to view it.
potential_rationale: This may improve code safety and API design by preventing unintended modifications to block index data, which could help reduce bugs and make the kernel interface more robust for external users.

title: kernel: trim Chain interface
summary: Removes two functions from the kernel Chain interface (btck_chain_get_genesis and btck_chain_get_tip) that can be replaced with the more general btck_chain_get_by_height function. This simplifies the API by reducing redundant methods.
potential_rationale: This likely helps streamline the kernel interface by reducing API surface area, which may make it easier to maintain and less prone to inconsistencies. A simpler interface could also be more attractive to external projects using the kernel.

title: kernel: add btck_block_tree_entry_equals
summary: Adds a comparison function for BlockTreeEntry objects in the kernel library to allow clients to easily check if two entries are equal.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves the usability of Bitcoin's kernel library for external developers by providing a standardized way to compare blockchain tree entries, which may help reduce code duplication and potential bugs in client implementations.

title: kernel: handle null or empty directories in implementation
summary: Modifies the kernel library to properly handle null or empty directory paths instead of requiring non-null arguments.
potential_rationale: This appears to make the kernel library more robust by gracefully handling edge cases with directory paths, which may help prevent crashes in client applications that use the library and could improve the overall stability of Bitcoin integrations.

title: kernel: don't use assert to handle invalid user input
summary: Replaces assert statements with proper error handling for invalid user input in the kernel library.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves the reliability of Bitcoin's kernel library for external integrations by allowing applications to gracefully handle errors instead of crashing, which may be particularly important for language bindings and third-party Bitcoin implementations.

title: log: exempt all category-specific logs from ratelimiting when running with debug
summary: Exempts all category-specific log messages from rate limiting when debug mode is enabled for that category.
potential_rationale: This appears to make Bitcoin Core's logging more consistent and useful for developers by ensuring that when they specifically request debug information for a category, all relevant messages are shown without artificial suppression, which may help with debugging and development workflows.
